Subject: Attached Request from Fresno District Attorney 
The attached letter appears to be in the nature of an 
opinion request. 
Do you feel it should be considered 
as such at the next meeting? 
In the alternative should 
I refer it to the Conflicts Division for review or should 
I respond to it myself? 
Please let me know how you feel 
this should be handled and if further information should 
be obtained from Kiraly before we respond. 


BRINTON N. BOWLES 
ASSISTANT DISTRICT ATTORNEY 
DENNIS CAETON 
ASSISTANT DISTRICT ATTORNEY 
OFFICE OF 
THE DISTRICT ATTORNEY 
COUNTY OF FRESNO 
WILLIAM A. SMITH 
DISTRICT ATTORNEY 
!ll[arch 15, 1977 
~r. Frederic M. Banelt 
Director, Enforcement Division 
Fair Political Practices Coamission 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, California 
95804 
Re: 
Political Reform Act and Conflict of Interest Code 
Dear Fred: 
COUNTY COUR mOUSE 
1100 VAN NESS AVENUE 
FRESNO, CALIFORNIA 93721 
PHONE: (209) 488-3141 
Reference is made to my letter dated February 16, 1977 
concerning a matter involving the Conflict of Interest Code. 
As indicated before, in order to comply with the Conflict 
of Interest Code section of the Reform Act we have re-organized 
our office so that only a certain amount of individuals, both 
attorneys and investigators, will handle the type of cases involved 
in these situations. 
This unit, which we will call our Consumer 
Fraud Unit, 
11 handle all the cases from complaint stage to 
trial stage. 
The attorneys in this unit will be making all 
decisions as to the matters at hand. 
There will be times, however, 
when it might be necessary to utilize other members of the Fresno 
County District Attorney's Office merely regarding a continuance 
to be handled in court or other similar minor, non-decisional 
court matters. 
We contemplate that the Consumer Fraud attorneys 
will be involved with continuances or whatever matters are to be 
handled, but may have to ask from a practical point to have others 
appear in court on those inconsequential or relatively minor type 
court matters. 
The regular staff members of the District Attorney's Office 
will not handle any of the court appearances so far as trials are 
concerned, or legal arguments or hearings, and will be making no 
decisions regarding these matters. 
The purpose of my bringing this factual situation to you 
1S to get your opinion as to whether or not I am correct in my 
interpretation. 
It seems that these supplemental assisting 
attorneys who merely appear for arraignment of an accused or 
continuance or the like would not 
involved in the decision 
making. 
, there 
, would not be required to make a financial 
disclosure statement. 
Is this correct? 


Mr. Frederic M. Hanelt 
March 15, 1977 
Page Two 
A written reply for our records would be greatly 
appreciated. 
Very truly yours, 
WILLIAM A. SMITH 
~
~resno County

sections.question: Whether supplemental assisting attorneys in the Fresno County District Attorney's Office who appear only for arraignments, continuances, or other minor non-decisional court matters need to make financial disclosure statements or be included in the Conflict of Interest Code, given that they will not be involved in decision-making regarding Consumer Fraud cases.
sections.conclusion: Based upon the facts set out above and your letter of March 15, 1977, we are of the opinion that supplemental assisting non-Consumer Fraud attorneys, operating in the manner prescribed, should not be included in the Conflict of Interest Code of the District Attorney's Office by virtue of their consequential or minor involvement with the division of Consumer Fraud.
sections.question_synthetic: The Fresno District Attorney asks whether non-Consumer Fraud unit attorneys who handle only minor court appearances (arraignments, continuances) need to be included in the Conflict of Interest Code and file financial disclosures.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC concludes that supplemental attorneys handling only minor court matters are not required to be included in the Conflict of Interest Code due to their inconsequential involvement with Consumer Fraud cases.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document quality is poor with significant scanning/OCR artifacts and missing text throughout. The question and conclusion sections are identifiable but fragmented. An internal memorandum is also included (dated March 23, 1977) discussing how to handle the request. The FPPC response letter contains the formal conclusion but key passages are degraded.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether supplemental assisting attorneys in the Fresno County District Attorney's Office who appear only for arraignments, continuances, or other minor non-decisional court matters need to make financial disclosure statements or be included in the Conflict of Interest Code, given that they will not be involved in decision-making regarding Consumer Fraud cases.

CONCLUSION: Based upon the facts set out above and your letter of March 15, 1977, we are of the opinion that supplemental assisting non-Consumer Fraud attorneys, operating in the manner prescribed, should not be included in the Conflict of Interest Code of the District Attorney's Office by virtue of their consequential or minor involvement with the division of Consumer Fraud.

embedding.summary: The Fresno District Attorney inquires whether supplemental attorneys who appear only for minor court matters in Consumer Fraud cases need to be included in the Conflict of Interest Code. FPPC advises that such attorneys need not be included due to their minor, non-decisional involvement.
